assertions.title: Handle Merge Conflicts

steps.action: Build the public `@codex-fleet/git-ops` package surface, provision an isolated divergent origin/repo-under-test/peer sandbox under `/tmp/codex-fleet-user-testing-val-git-010-rerun` on `shuvtest`, and set `git config pull.rebase false` in `repo-under-test`.
steps.expected: The repo-under-test is one commit ahead of `origin/main` with no existing merge state so a subsequent pull will exercise a real content-conflict path.
steps.observed: Setup completed successfully: `repo-under-test` was ahead of `origin/main` by one local commit and `origin/main` still pointed at the base commit before the API call.

steps.action: Call `GitOps.pull(host, repoPath)` through the public package API via `node --input-type=module` from the repo root.
steps.expected: The API returns a typed `MergeConflict` error whose `files` list includes the conflicted path (`conflict.txt`).
steps.observed: The API failed in 304 ms, but returned `_tag=GitCommandFailed` for `git pull origin` instead of `MergeConflict`, with no conflicted file list in the error payload.

steps.action: Verify the repository directly with raw git/SSH after the API failure.
steps.expected: The repo remains in a recoverable merge-conflict state and direct git identifies the same conflicted files the API reported.
steps.observed: Direct verification showed `UU conflict.txt`, `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U` returned `conflict.txt`, `MERGE_HEAD` existed, and local HEAD stayed unchanged, but the API reported no conflicted files.

MERGE_HEAD_PRESENT=yes

assertions.issues: The public API did not satisfy the contract. After a real merge-conflict pull, direct git reported `conflict.txt` in a recoverable merge state, but `GitOps.pull` surfaced `_tag=GitCommandFailed` and did not report the conflicted file list.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 0 passed, 1 failed, 0 blocked. `GitOps.pull` left the repo in a recoverable conflict state with `conflict.txt`, but the public API returned `GitCommandFailed` instead of a `MergeConflict` containing the conflicted file list.
